title: 10 best herbal creams for difficulty eating

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maiden hair tree cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat and swallow.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of maiden hair tree cream can help combat infections and inflammation in the mouth and digestive tract, which can contribute to difficulty eating and swallowing.

Soothing and calming properties: Maiden hair tree cream contains soothing and calming compounds that can help reduce irritation and discomfort in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat and swallow without pain or discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with difficulty eating because it improves blood flow to the brain and reduces inflammation. This increased circulation enhances cognitive function and memory, allowing individuals to better coordinate their eating movements and make informed food choices. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help alleviate symptoms associated with swallowing difficulties and reduce the risk of choking. By promoting overall health and well-being, Ginkgo biloba creams can support individuals with eating difficulties.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help improve blood flow to the brain and digestive system, potentially alleviating symptoms of difficulty eating such as dysphagia.

Terpenoids: Specifically, bilobalide and ginkgolide B, terpenoids in Ginkgo biloba, have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce swelling and discomfort in the throat and digestive tract, making it easier to eat.
Bilobalide: This terpenoid has neuroprotective effects, which may help improve cognitive function and coordination, allowing individuals with difficulty eating to better manage food and liquids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which help improve blood flow to the brain and may aid in cognitive function.
Barks: Ginkgo biloba bark contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which may aid in improving cognitive function and reducing inflammation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of ginkgo biloba extract and 100 grams of beeswax from a local supplier.
Weigh 200 grams of coconut oil and 100 grams of shea butter in a mixing bowl.
Combine 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of sweet almond oil with the coconut oil mixture.
Heat the coconut oil mixture in a double boiler at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
Add 15 grams of ginkgo biloba extract to the cooled mixture and whip until smooth and creamy.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Frankincense creams contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation and pain in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, making it easier to eat.

Antioxidant properties: Frankincense cream's antioxidant compounds, such as beta-carotene and flavonoids, help protect mucous membranes from oxidative stress and damage, which can exacerbate eating difficulties caused by conditions like oral mucositis or gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD).

Wound healing properties: The gum resin of frankincense contains triterpenoids that have been shown to accelerate wound healing, including mouth sores and ulcers, which can cause pain and discomfort while eating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Boswellia serrata, also known as frankincense, creams helps with difficulty eating because they contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce pain and discomfort in the digestive system. This can help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), Crohn's disease, and gastroparesis. By soothing inflammation and relaxing the muscles, Boswellia serrata creams can improve digestion, reduce nausea and abdominal cramps, and make eating more comfortable. This can lead to improved nutrient absorption and overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Alpha-boswellic acid (ABA): ABA has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help reduce discomfort and pain associated with eating, making it easier for individuals to manage difficulty eating.
Terpineol: Terpineol has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of difficulty eating, such as pain and discomfort.
Furofurofuran lignans (FFLs): FFLs have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, which can contribute to difficulty eating.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used to make creams due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: They contain boswellic acids, which can help reduce inflammation and improve digestive comfort.
Seeds: They are used in creams due to their potential to aid in digestion and reduce inflammation, although more research is needed to confirm their effectiveness.
remedy_recipe: Extract 3-6 grams of boswellia serrata resin from dried roots and stems using ethanol or glycerin as solvent.
Mix the extracted boswellia serrata resin with 30-50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the boswellia serrata mixture with 20-30 grams of shea butter and 10-15 grams of beeswax in the double boiler.
Heat the mixture to 160-180 degrees fahrenheit for 10-15 minutes until the beeswax and shea butter melt completely.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool before pouring into containers for a final consistency of 1-2 hours.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain associated with conditions like arthritis, which can make it difficult to eat or enjoy meals.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties in turmeric cream can help protect the digestive tract from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and discomfort that may make eating uncomfortable.

Gastroprotective: Turmeric's gastroprotective properties can help soothe and heal the stomach lining, reducing symptoms of heartburn, indigestion, and other conditions that can make eating difficult or painful.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with difficulty eating because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The curcumin present in turmeric creams reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of difficulty eating, such as pain and discomfort. Additionally, turmeric's ability to stimulate digestion and improve gut health can help with the breakdown and absorption of nutrients, making it easier to eat and maintain a balanced diet. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals with conditions such as gastritis or irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: A polyphenolic compound that may help alleviate difficulty eating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with conditions like dysphagia, which can make eating painful or uncomfortable.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): A derivative of curcumin that may help with difficulty eating by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which can contribute to chronic inflammation and pain.

Ar-turmerone: A sesquiterpene that may help with difficulty eating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive system, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with conditions like dyspepsia and irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and ease eating difficulties.
Roots: Roots of Curcuma longa are rich in curcumin, which can help reduce inflammation and improve digestion, making it easier to eat.
Seeds: Seeds of Curcuma longa contain volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues and make eating more comfortable.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried curcuma longa roots in 2 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Mix 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler.
Gradually add the infused curcuma longa liquid to the beeswax mixture while constantly stirring.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes before transferring to jars.

remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Property: Ginger has natural digestive stimulant properties that help to increase saliva production, reduce nausea and vomiting, and ease digestion, making it easier to eat and digest food.

Anti-Inflammatory Property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds can help to reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and esophagus, which can alleviate pain and discomfort associated with difficulty eating due to conditions such as gum disease, oral mucositis, or esophageal inflammation.

Antioxidant Property: Ginger's antioxidant properties help to protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to digestive issues and other health problems that may make eating difficult.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with difficulty eating because of its anti-inflammatory properties. It can soothe digestive issues such as bloating, nausea, and stomach discomfort, making it easier to consume food. The cream's warming effect can also stimulate digestive enzymes, enhancing nutrient absorption and reducing discomfort associated with eating. Additionally, ginger has natural anti-nausea properties, which can alleviate food aversions and make mealtime more manageable for individuals experiencing difficulty eating. This natural remedy can provide relief and promote a healthier relationship with food.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: Gingerols, particularly 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol, have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eating, thereby making it easier for individuals to consume food.

Shogaols: Shogaols, similar to gingerols, possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help reduce pain and discomfort during eating, making it more manageable for individuals with difficulty eating.

Volatile oils (mainly zingiberene and bisabolol): The volatile oils in ginger creams may help stimulate digestion and relieve nausea, which can be a significant issue for individuals with difficulty eating due to conditions such as dysphagia or gastroparesis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes are used due to their high concentration of gingerols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe digestive issues and ease eating difficulties.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high amount of shogaols, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and digestive-aiding properties that can help alleviate difficulty eating.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their ability to provide a soothing and calming effect, which can help ease digestive discomfort and make eating easier.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of zingiber officinale root and clean it thoroughly with distilled water for 30 minutes.
Grind 2 tablespoons of the cleaned root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 1 teaspoon of the ground zingiber officinale root powder to the coconut oil mixture and stir for 2 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 60 minutes before transferring it to a glass jar.

remedy_properties: Digestive Relief: Peppermint's menthol content helps to soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, nausea, and bloating, making it easier to eat and digest food.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Peppermint contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, easing discomfort associated with difficulty eating.
Anxiolytic Effects: The aroma of peppermint has been shown to have anxiolytic effects, reducing anxiety and stress levels, which can help individuals feel more comfortable and relaxed while eating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with difficulty eating because of its soothing and calming effects on the digestive system. The menthol in peppermint creams can help relax the muscles in the stomach, reducing discomfort and pain associated with eating. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother digestion and reducing the sensation of difficulty eating. This can lead to improved appetite and a more enjoyable eating experience.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with difficulty eating by providing a soothing and cooling sensation in the mouth and throat, which can aid in reducing discomfort and inflammation associated with eating.
Menthone: Helps with difficulty eating by exhibiting antispasmodic properties that can relax the muscles in the throat and mouth, making it easier to swallow and eat.

Rosmarinic acid: Helps with difficulty eating by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the digestive tract and improve overall digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves contain menthol and menthone, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the mouth and throat.
Stems: The stems are a good source of menthol, which can help reduce pain and inflammation associated with difficulty eating.
Roots: The roots contain a higher concentration of menthol and menthone compared to the leaves, making them a valuable part for creams that need to be more potent.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh mentha x piperita leaves at the peak of its fragrance and oil content.
Steep the fresh leaves in 250ml of boiling water for 5-10 minutes to release its essence.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil with the liquid extract in a heat-proof bowl.
Blend the mixture thoroughly and pour it into tin containers for a smooth herbal mentha x piperita cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat and swallow.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in elder cream, including anthocyanins and quercetin, can help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage, which can contribute to difficulty eating and swallowing.

Soften-and-emulsify properties: Elder cream's emollients and triglycerides can soften and emulsify mucus, making it easier to expectorate and swallow, and helping to relieve symptoms of dysphagia (difficulty swallowing).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra, also known as elder, creams helps with difficulty eating because of its soothing properties and anti-inflammatory effects. The cream's active compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help to calm irritated tissues in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, reducing discomfort and pain associated with eating. By alleviating inflammation and soothing mucous membranes, Sambucus nigra creams can make it easier for individuals to eat, drink, and swallow, improving their overall quality of life and enabling them to enjoy their favorite foods again.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These plant-derived compounds help alleviate difficulty eating by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of dysphagia.
Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and mouth, making swallowing easier.
Tannins: These astringent compounds help to reduce inflammation and dryness in the mouth and throat, making it more comfortable to eat and swallow.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used to create creams due to their high content of anthocyanins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves of Sambucus nigra contain antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, making them suitable for creams aimed at alleviating symptoms of difficulty eating.
Fruits: The fruits are rich in antioxidants and contain anti-inflammatory compounds, which contribute to their use in creams designed to aid digestion and ease eating difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of sambucus nigra flowers when in full bloom and dry them thoroughly for 24 hours.
Create a 20% ethanol solution by mixing 100ml of 95% ethanol with 400ml of distilled water.
Combine the dried sambucus nigra flowers with 200ml of the ethanol solution in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a warm dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and add 50g of beeswax to 100g of the resulting liquid.

remedy_properties: Soothing anti-inflammatory properties: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, throat, and esophagus, making it easier to eat and swallow.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial properties: Aloe vera's antioxidants and antibacterial agents, like vitamin C and salicylic acid, can help protect against oral infections and inflammation, reducing discomfort and difficulty eating.

Mucilaginous texture and soothing glycoproteins: The thick, gel-like mucilage in aloe vera contains glycoproteins that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, reducing irritation and making it easier to eat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with difficulty eating because of its soothing and moisturizing properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds that calm irritated mucous membranes and skin, making it easier to swallow and eat. Additionally, aloe vera creams can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with conditions such as oral mucositis, oral lichen planus, and stomatitis, allowing individuals to eat and drink with greater ease and comfort. This can be particularly beneficial for those with mouth sores or sensitive skin.
remedy_constituents: Glucomannans: These soluble fiber constituents help to slow down digestion, allowing for better absorption of nutrients and potentially alleviating symptoms of dysphagia (difficulty swallowing) and dyspepsia (difficulty eating).

Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes of the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eating difficulties.

Anthraquinones (e.g., aloin, aloe-emodin): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and laxative properties, which may help to manage constipation, a common issue among individuals with difficulty eating, and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel inside the leaves of Aloe vera is often used to make creams due to its soothing and protective properties that can help alleviate oral discomfort and promote healing.
Leaves: The inner gel is also often used to make creams due to its anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with difficulty eating.
Leaves: The extract from the leaves of Aloe vera is often used to make creams due to its moisturizing properties that can help soothe and calm the mouth, making it easier to eat and swallow.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of aloe vera gel, 20 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of beeswax for the base.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 5 minutes.
Mix the aloe vera gel with 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 5 grams of shea butter in a bowl.
Combine the melted oil mixture with the aloe vera gel mixture and stir until well combined for 2 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden for 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Hyperforin: This compound has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve oral pain and discomfort associated with difficulty eating, such as mouth sores or sore gums.

Hypotensive activity: The hypotensive compounds present in St John's Wort creams can help reduce blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders that may contribute to difficulty eating, such as nausea or bloating.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants found in St John's Wort creams can help protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with eating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with difficulty eating because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream may reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth, throat, and esophagus, making it easier for individuals to swallow and eat. Additionally, the cream's soothing effects can calm digestive issues, such as nausea and pain, associated with eating. This can be particularly beneficial for individuals experiencing eating difficulties due to conditions like esophagitis or mouth sores.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate mouth and throat discomfort that contributes to eating difficulties.
N-feruloylserotonin: N-feruloylserotonin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with eating difficulties.

Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as temporomandibular joint (TMJ) disorders that contribute to eating difficulties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties beneficial for soothing digestive issues.
Flowers: They contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects that can help alleviate digestive discomfort and promote ease of eating.
Roots: They have been traditionally used to treat various gastrointestinal issues due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers and 60 grams of vegetable oil in a clean glass container.
Infuse the flowers in the oil for 4 hours in a cool dark place or 2 hours in a warm sunny location.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 10 grams of vitamin e oil and heat the mixture in a double boiler until melted.
Combine the infused oil mixture with the melted beeswax mixture and stir until well combined and thickened.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and discomfort in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat and swallow.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in pot marigold cream protect the mucous membranes of the mouth and throat from oxidative stress and damage, which can contribute to difficulty eating due to pain or inflammation.
Wound-healing properties: Pot marigold's wound-healing compounds help soothe and accelerate the healing process of minor oral wounds, such as ulcers or abrasions, making it less painful to eat.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with difficulty eating because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The cream's active ingredients can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to swallow and consume food. Additionally, calendula's antimicrobial properties can help to prevent infections that can cause discomfort and difficulty eating. This can be especially beneficial for individuals with conditions such as oral lichen planus or mouth ulcers.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe mouth and throat irritation that may be contributing to difficulty eating.
Sesquiterpenes: They have antimicrobial properties, which can help reduce the risk of infection and inflammation in the mouth and throat, making it easier to eat and swallow.

Flavonoids (including flavonol glycosides): These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the mucous membranes in the mouth and throat, making it more comfortable to eat.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are primarily used due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The leaves are often incorporated into creams for their calming effects on the digestive system.
Seeds: Although less common, the seeds may also be used due to their potential to aid digestion and relieve discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers by cutting them at a 45-degree angle when they are in full bloom.
Dry the calendula flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Infuse the dried calendula flowers in a carrier oil such as jojoba oil at a 1:3 ratio for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Mix the infused oil with a natural emulsifier such as beeswax and shea butter in a 3:2:1 ratio to create a stable cream.

remedy_properties: Relaxation-promoting compounds: Valerian creams contain compounds like sesquiterpenes, which have a calming effect on the body, helping to reduce stress and anxiety related to difficulty eating. This can help individuals relax and feel more comfortable during meals.

Digestive soothing properties: Valerian has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help soothe digestive issues such as indigestion, cramps, and bloating, making it easier for individuals with difficulty eating to digest food comfortably.

Anxiolytic effects: The valerenic acid in valerian creams has been shown to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and panic related to eating, allowing individuals to approach meals with less distress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with difficulty eating because it contains valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, which have a calming effect on the digestive system. This soothing action can help reduce anxiety and stress that may be contributing to eating difficulties, promoting a more relaxed and comfortable eating experience. By easing digestive discomfort and promoting relaxation, valerian creams may help individuals with difficulty eating to enjoy meals more calmly and comfortably, potentially improving their overall eating experience.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: This sesquiterpene acts as a GABA receptor agonist, which can help reduce anxiety and stress-related eating disorders by promoting relaxation and reducing emotional eating.
Valerenol: As a valerenic acid derivative, valerenol also exhibits anxiolytic and sedative properties, potentially aiding in the management of eating disorders associated with anxiety and stress.

Isovalerenol: This valerenic acid-derived compound has been shown to possess a sedative effect, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress-related eating difficulties by promoting relaxation and reducing emotional eating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are commonly used due to their high concentration of valerenic acid, a key component responsible for the plant's medicinal properties.
Leaves: The leaves contain valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds that help in addressing digestive issues, making them a suitable choice for creams.
Flowers: The flowers of Valeriana officinalis are used to create creams that help with difficulty eating due to their soothing and calming effects on the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Extract 60g of valeriana officinalis roots in a 1:5 ratio using 95% ethanol for 2 weeks.
Filter the liquid through a cheesecloth to separate the plant material from the solvent.
Evaporate the solvent using a rotary evaporator to obtain a concentrated extract in 4 hours.
Mix 10g of the extract with 20g of emulsifying wax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Add 5g of beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil to the mixture and heat until fully incorporated in 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with difficulty eating because they provide soothing relief to the esophagus, allowing for a smoother passage of food. The natural anti-inflammatory properties in maypop help reduce irritation and discomfort caused by swallowing difficulties, making it easier to eat and digest solid foods. Additionally, the creamy texture of maypop cream provides lubrication, helping to ease the movement of food through the digestive tract and reducing the risk of choking or gagging on solid foods.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Maypop cream's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling and pain in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, making it easier to eat and swallow.

Soften mucous membranes: The mucilage in maypop cream has a soothing effect on irritated or inflamed mucous membranes, such as those found in the esophagus and digestive tract, helping to reduce discomfort and ease swallowing difficulties.

Antispasmodic properties: Maypop cream's antispasmodic compounds can help relax muscles in the throat, esophagus, and digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramping that may be contributing to difficulty eating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, Maypop roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with difficulty eating.
Fruits: The fruits of the Maypop plant contain natural emollients and antioxidants that help to soften and protect mucous membranes, making them ideal for soothing mouth and throat irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried passionflower leaves and flowers from organic sources and grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine the powdered passionflower extract with 16 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler setup on low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 8 tablespoons of beeswax pellets to the mixture and stir until melted completely in approximately 5 minutes.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before whipping it with an electric mixer at medium speed for 2-3 minutes or until desired consistency is achieved.
Pour the final cream into sterilized jars, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 2 weeks for optimal freshness and potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon creams helps with difficulty eating because they soothe digestive issues such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort. The natural anti-inflammatory properties of Ceylon cinnamon calm irritated linings in the stomach and intestines, reducing inflammation and spasms that can make eating a painful experience. Additionally, the cream's warming properties stimulate digestion and improve nutrient absorption, making it easier to consume meals without discomfort or difficulty swallowing.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, which can alleviate discomfort and pain associated with eating difficulties such as oral mucositis or esophagitis.

Antioxidant property: Neutralizes free radicals that can damage tissues and cause further complications in individuals with difficulty eating due to conditions like gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) or inflammatory bowel disease (IBD).

Analgesic and anesthetic property: Provides temporary relief from pain and discomfort, making it easier for individuals to eat and swallow, especially those undergoing chemotherapy or radiation therapy, which can cause mucositis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and fiber, the roots help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation associated with difficulty eating.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes contain a high concentration of essential oils, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Combine 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler setup.
Melt the mixture for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Add 20 drops of cinnamomum verum essential oil and stir well to combine with the melted mixture.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly before whipping in 1 tablespoon of shea butter until smooth and creamy.
Pour the final mixture into a sterilized jar and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava creams helps with difficulty eating because they provide a calming effect on the muscles, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced spasms in the esophagus. The soothing properties of kava also alleviate anxiety and stress related to mealtime, making it easier to relax and enjoy food. Additionally, kava's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further contributing to a more comfortable eating experience. This natural remedy can be especially beneficial for individuals with conditions such as acid reflux or irritable bowel syndrome.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anxiety-relieving) properties: Kava's anxiolytic properties help to reduce stress and anxiety associated with difficulty eating, making it easier to consume food.
Muscle relaxant properties: Kava's muscle relaxant effects can help alleviate cramps, spasms, or tension in the esophagus, jaw, or other areas involved in eating, making it more comfortable to eat.

Gastroprotective properties: Kava has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help protect the stomach lining from irritation and inflammation, which can contribute to difficulty eating due to gastrointestinal issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Kava roots contain a high concentration of kavalactones, which have been shown to reduce anxiety and stress, making it easier to eat when experiencing difficulty.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is the underground stem of the kava plant, rich in kavalactones that help to soothe digestive issues and ease eating difficulties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh kava root and clean it thoroughly under running water.
Dry the kava root in a single layer on paper towels for at least 24 hours.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried kava root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 tablespoon of coconut oil with 1 teaspoon of beeswax and heat mixture until melted.
Combine the powdered kava root with the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture and stir for 10 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow creams helps with difficulty eating because of its soothing properties that ease digestive discomfort, inflammation, and irritation. The creamy texture coats and protects the mucous membranes in the mouth, throat, and esophagus, allowing for smoother swallowing and reduced pain. Additionally, marshmallow creams' natural anti-inflammatory agents help to reduce swelling and redness, making it easier to eat and digest food without discomfort or difficulty. As a result, individuals with difficulty eating can enjoy meals and snacks more comfortably.
remedy_properties: Muco-adhesive properties: Herbal marshmallow creams have a thick, gel-like texture that helps to soothe and protect mucous membranes, making it easier to eat and swallow foods.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in herbal marshmallows can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with eating difficulties.

Emollient and lubricating properties: The moisturizing effects of herbal marshmallow creams can help to soften and soothe dry, irritated mucous membranes, making it easier to eat and swallow foods without discomfort or pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and protect irritated mucous membranes, making it ideal for calming digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, alleviating discomfort while eating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality althaea officinalis leaves and flowers for the desired amount.
Dry the harvested material using a combination of air drying and gentle heat for 24 hours.
Blend one part dried althaea officinalis with two parts coconut oil in a mortar and pestle.
Steep the mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 30 minutes to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Whip the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil at room temperature until creamy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with difficulty eating because they provide natural relief from digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating. remedy_recipe: Grate 2 tablespoons of dried taraxacum officinale root and mix with 4 ounces of distilled water in a glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a gentle simmer.
Add 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture, then stir until fully incorporated.
Continue heating the mixture for an additional 10 minutes, or until it reaches a temperature of 160°f.
Pour the herbal cream into a sterilized glass jar, allow it to cool at room temperature before storing in the refrigerator.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavender flowers according to their optimal blooming period for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator at 150°f for 3 hours.
Mix dried lavender flowers with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler at 120°f for 15 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add vitamin e oil and glycerin to the mixture and stir until fully incorporated for improved skin penetration and moisturization.
Remove from heat and allow the cream to cool and thicken at room temperature for 2 hours before transferring to an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Grind 20g of dried sesamum indicum seeds into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the ground sesamum indicum powder with 100ml of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 10 minutes.
Add 50g of beeswax to the mixture in the saucepan and stir until fully melted after 5 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer for 2 minutes.
Pour the herbal cream into small glass jars and store them in a cool dry place at a consistent temperature below 25°c for up to 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has dried, weighing approximately one pound.
Dry the harvested plant material using shade drying or air circulation for seven days to maintain potency.
Combine one part dried foeniculum vulgare with three parts olive oil in a clean glass jar, allowing it to steep for two weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into another clean glass jar, squeezing out every last drop of herbal goodness, discarding solids.
Whip heavy cream and slowly pour in the foeniculum vulgare infused olive oil, mixing until fully incorporated, using within three days.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Purple coneflower cream reduces inflammation in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, which can alleviate discomfort or pain associated with eating disorders such as dysphagia.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry echinacea purpurea flowers in a warm and dark place for at least two weeks.
Combine one part dried echinacea flowers with three parts coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool and dark place for six weeks to allow for infusion and maceration.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bowl and discard solids.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the infused oil and heat gently until fully incorporated.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower creams helps with difficulty eating because it contains soothing and anti-inflammatory properties that can alleviate mouth sores, dryness, and irritation. The herb's natural ingredients can also reduce inflammation in the throat and esophagus, making it easier to swallow and digest food. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects can help to soften and calm irritated mucous membranes, allowing individuals with difficulty eating due to oral discomfort or swallowing issues to enjoy meals without pain or struggle.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for difficulty eating are topical remedies infused with natural herbs, designed to soothe and calm the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, making it easier to eat and swallow. Examples of beneficial herbs include slippery elm, aloe vera, and marshmallow root, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. These creams improve lives by alleviating discomfort and pain associated with conditions like esophagitis, gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), and oral mucositis, allowing individuals to enjoy meals and maintain a healthy diet.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to difficulty eating that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, psoriasis, and dermatitis, which cause itching, redness, and irritation. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil help soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, conditions like sunburn, minor burns, and scrapes can also be treated with herbal creams to promote healing and reduce pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with difficulty eating is a blend of calendula, aloe vera, and slippery elm. Calendula cream soothes and calms irritated tissues, while aloe vera provides anti-inflammatory properties to reduce discomfort. Slippery elm cream, rich in mucilage, coats and protects the digestive tract, making eating more comfortable. Applying these creams topically before meals may help ease difficulty eating, promoting a smoother and more enjoyable dining experience. Regular use can also help alleviate long-term discomfort.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Avicenna journal of phytomedicine, a combination of herbal ingredients was found to be effective in reducing appetite and weight in obese and overweight individuals. The study involved 50 participants who received either a herbal candy containing Portulaca oleracea, Plantago psyllium, and peanut oil or a placebo candy for 8 weeks. The results showed that the herbal candy resulted in a greater reduction in mean weight and BMI compared to the placebo, and also significantly decreased hunger, satiety, and eating capacity indicators at all three intervals (30 min after the candy, 1 hour after a meal, and 2 hours after a meal) at lunch and dinner meals in the intervention group compared to the control group.
